questions.sentence: While basic Flexbox usage is simple, mastering its alignment properties allows you to build complex layouts with ease.
Two key properties to understand are 'justify-content' and 'align-items'.
'Justify-content' controls the alignment of items along the main axis.
On the other hand, 'align-items' handles alignment along the cross axis, which is the vertical alignment in a row.
For example, using 'center' for both properties will perfectly center an element inside its container.
Additionally, the 'flex-direction' property allows you to switch the main axis from a row to a column, making Flexbox an incredibly versatile tool for both mobile and desktop designs.
